previewModes.authored: Uses an asset-specific reconstruction when available, otherwise follows each assigned material's active Blender Material Output and maps constant surface inputs to Three.js.
previewModes.diagnostic: Uses the shared pale-green material so geometry, normals, silhouettes, and topology can be compared without shader differences.

assetSpecificReconstructions.automaticRecognition: Strict graph-and-attribute recognition also applies the attribute-emission and Chrome Crayon reconstructions to compatible materials in default catalog assets.
implementations: attribute-driven Principled color, roughness, and metallic
N03D filament color, layer bands, rough plastic, and backface treatment
joint-generator filament color, roughness, and layer fields
joint procedural mahogany Wave/Noise ramps and Principled coat

portableShaderMetadata.contents: Fresh Blender 5.1 extraction with Color Ramp elements and internal ShaderNodeTree definitions; merged into the four joint assets without rewriting their validated geometry dumps.

limitations: Linked procedural inputs currently use their constant fallback and are labeled as approximations on the Three.js material contract.
Mix Shader, shader-group, texture, coordinate-mapping, bump, and displacement networks require focused WebGL reconstructions.
The shared browser environment approximates Blender studio/render lighting; authored mode is material-semantic parity, not pixel-identical Cycles or Eevee rendering.
